name: plan-guard
description: Sidecar plugin that enforces step-by-step plan locked-decision adherence — the marker + hook + linter triad that closes the three observed plan-drift root causes (orchestrator escape in sub-agent prompts · mid-design drift off the original plan · sbs Auto-QA conformance being a soft self-check that misses it). CORE (0.1.0): the `plan-lint` engine (`_plan_lint.hexa`, via `hexa run`) parses a plan.md's `## locked decisions` `@L<n> … assert:<kind> <arg>` machine-readable contract (step-by-step 0.8.0 marker spec) and verifies each assert against the repo or, with `--diff <files…>`, a restricted file set: `grep <pat>` (term present · `!pat` = absent) · `file <path>` (exists) · `verdict <slug>/<id>` (`.verdicts/<slug>/<id>.txt` non-empty). Prints a per-`@L` ✓/✗ verdict and exits NONZERO when any assert is unmet; a plan with no `@L` lines is skipped (exit 0, backward-compatible). Advisory by design (the linter exits nonzero so a caller can surface it, but the bundled hooks only ever emit additionalContext — never deny). Mirrors the memory-lint hexa-lint pattern (defensive readers, JSON-escaped additionalContext, no network). 0.2.0 adds the PreToolUse(Task|Agent) inject hook (`_plan_inject.hexa`): on every sub-agent spawn it resolves the ACTIVE plan (drafts/*-plan.md with frontmatter `status: active`, else newest-mtime fallback), extracts its `@L` locked-decision lines, and injects them VERBATIM as additionalContext so the orchestrator cannot silently write a contradicting escape into the spawn prompt (root cause #1 — the T2/d1 '.kosmos 필수' → 'SKIP OK' incident); it also scans the spawn prompt for escape language (skip/ignore/생략/… near a locked term or human-label token) and appends an advisory warning when a possible contradiction is found. Advisory-only — never denies, always exits 0. 0.3.0 adds the PostToolUse(Bash) ship-lint hook (`_plan_ship.hexa`): fires after a ship command (`git commit` / `gh pr …`), resolves the ACTIVE plan, runs the PR-2 plan-lint engine (single source of truth) against the shipped diff (`git diff origin/main...HEAD` file set, else staged/HEAD, else repo-wide) via `--diff`, and surfaces any UNMET `@L` asserts as a verbatim additionalContext warning so a mid-design drift off the locked plan (root cause #2, e.g. 'AKIDA-first → GPU') is caught at ship — where sbs Auto-QA's soft conformance self-check (root cause #3) missed it. Non-blocking — the ship already happened; the warning rides the next turn. Always exits 0. 0.3.1 hardens active-plan resolution (@L6): the newest-mtime fallback now SKIPS plans explicitly closed (`status: done` / `abandoned`), so once a plan is flipped done at closure its `@L` is never enforced against a later unrelated session — paired with the step-by-step 0.8.1 closure flip (`active` → `done`/`abandoned`). 0.3.2 documents the dogfood self-test (the build plan's @L1~@L6 all PASS against the landed artifacts; 2 honest findings logged — a marker placeholder backtick parse-confusion and an over-broad repo-wide `!grep` that caught sibling guards' legitimate deny, reframed to a scope-correct assert).
